name: Thai Crab Rolls
recipeIngredient: 1/3 cup Fish sauce (see cook's notes, below)
2 tbsp Unsalted butter or olive oil
1 cup Lump crabmeat, fresh or drained canned, well picked over
1 tbsp Fish sauce (see cook's notes) or salt to taste
Freshly ground black pepper to taste
1 tbsp All-purpose flour mixed with 1 tablespoon cold vegetable oil
recipeInstructions:
Prepare the Sauce:
Combine vinegar and sugar in a small saucepan. Bring mixture to boil over moderate heat until sugar is dissolved. Transfer liquid to a small bowl. Add garlic, pepper, and fish sauce. Set aside.
Make the Filling:
Melt butter in skillet over moderate heat. Add garlic and onion. Cook until onion is translucent (about 2 minutes). Add celery, carrot, and mushrooms; saute vegetables until soft but not browned (about 2 minutes). Add crabmeat, fish sauce, and black pepper; toss well. Transfer filling to a bowl and cool completely.
Assemble the Rolls:
Stack spring roll wrappers. Cut stacked wrappers in half diagonally with sharp knife or scissors. Work one triangle at a time, keeping remaining wrappers covered with damp towel. Place smooth side down on work surface. Brush sheet lightly with flour paste. Fill lower edge of wrapper with 2 heaping teaspoons filling and fold points over filling. Roll up from bottom to enclose filling. Place roll seam-side down on plate; keep covered until all rolls are assembled.
Preheat Oven:
Set oven to 250F. Line baking sheet with paper towels.
Cook the Rolls:
Heat oil in wok or deep heavy skillet to 350F. Gently add rolls in batches, turning occasionally. Cook until golden brown (about 2 minutes). Remove with slotted spoon and drain on sieve over large bowl. Place rolls on baking sheet; keep warm in oven while cooking remaining rolls.
Serve:
Serve rolls with dipping sauce. Variation: Crabmeat Triangles - Cut each wrapper into 3 strips. Place filling on bottom corner of each strip, fold up and over flag-style to form triangles. Brush end with flour paste before final fold to seal. Fry as above or bake in preheated 375F oven until golden brown (about 15-20 minutes). Serve alone without sauce.
Cook's Notes: Fish sauce brands like Squid or Tiparos available at Asian markets or well-stocked supermarkets. Once opened, fish sauce lasts up to a year at room temperature. Crabmeat substitutes include cooked salmon, lobster meat, or chicken. For extra flavor, wrap triangles in lettuce leaves with mint and coriander sprigs before dipping. Yield: 20 rolls for hors d'oeuvres or 4-6 appetizer servings.
